free_text: Excitation functions of 3He- and a- particle induced
nuclear reactions on natSb for production of
medically relevant 123I and 124I radioisotopes
High purity natural antimony was vacuum-evaporated
onto 13mu-m thick polyimide(kapton,C22H10O5N2) and 9
mu-m Al backings. The thicknesses of Sb layers were
between 0.2 and 1.17 mu-m.
Six stacks were irradiated
with 3He-beam of 28MeV primary energy and addi-
tional three stacks were activated with a-beams of
20 and 21MeV primary energies for 2h using about
160nA beam current at the external beams of the
MGC-20 cyclotrons of Debrecen and Turku.Charge was
collected in Faraday-cups at both places.Ti foils of
13 and 22mu-m and Cu foils of 8 mu-m thicknesses
were inserted into the stacks and were used as
monitor foils and energy degraders.
Decay data were taken from NuDat 2.4.
Chapter 4
The uncertainty of the energy was estimated
from the following components:energy of the primary
incident beam and target thickness taking into
account the well known energy straggling effect.
The independent relative errors of the
linearly contributing processes were summed
quadratically and the square root was taken.
